description: Fix documentation inaccuracies found during a systematic audit of the docs against the source code. Covers missing AbortSignal documentation for SDK utilities, incorrect error-handling descriptions for storage functions, missing HTTP status code mappings, a missing config field, and undocumented class properties in the plugin development guide.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Document AbortSignal support for sleep() and waitForSelector()
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK utility docs to show the AbortSignal options for sleep() and waitForSelector() so that I can discover the cancellation capabilities of these functions.
acceptanceCriteria: The `sleep()` section in utilities.mdx shows the signature as `sleep(ms: number, opts?: SleepOptions): Promise<void>` with a SleepOptions table documenting the `signal` parameter (type `AbortSignal`, optional, description about cancelling sleep early)
The `waitForSelector()` section in utilities.mdx includes `signal` in the WaitForSelectorOptions table (type `AbortSignal`, optional, description about cancelling the wait early)
The `waitForSelectorRemoval()` section also includes `signal` in its options table if the source code supports it (check dom.ts WaitForSelectorRemovalOptions)
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in utilities.mdx is set to today's date (2026-02-28)
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Source of truth:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts lines 27-58: SleepOptions interface has `signal?: AbortSignal`. The sleep function checks signal.aborted and attaches an abort listener.
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts lines 11-16: WaitForSelectorOptions interface has `signal?: AbortSignal` alongside `timeout`.
- Also check WaitForSelectorRemovalOptions in dom.ts to see if it also has signal support.

Current state of docs:
- sleep() section (around line 652) shows signature as just `sleep(ms: number): Promise<void>` with no options parameter.
- waitForSelector() options table (around line 37) only lists `timeout` — no `signal` row.

Pattern to follow: Look at how retry() and waitUntil() document their `signal` option in the same file — they have a row in the options table with type AbortSignal and a description. Follow the same pattern for sleep and waitForSelector.

userStories.title: Fix storage utility error handling description and httpStatusToToolError table
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want accurate documentation of storage utility error behavior and HTTP status code mappings so that I can correctly handle errors in my plugin code.
acceptanceCriteria: The `setLocalStorage` description says 'Logs a warning via `log.warn()` if storage access throws' instead of 'Silently fails if storage access throws'
The `setSessionStorage` description says 'Logs a warning via `log.warn()` if storage access throws' instead of 'Silently fails if storage access throws'
The `removeLocalStorage` description says 'Logs a warning via `log.warn()` if storage access throws' instead of 'Silently fails if storage access throws'
The `removeSessionStorage` description says 'Logs a warning via `log.warn()` if storage access throws' instead of 'Silently fails if storage access throws'
The `httpStatusToToolError` status mapping table includes a row for `400, 422` mapping to category `validation` with `retryable: false`
The `httpStatusToToolError` status mapping table includes a row for `408` mapping to category `timeout` with `retryable: true`
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in utilities.mdx is set to today's date (2026-02-28)
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Source of truth for storage:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts lines 23-28: setLocalStorage catches errors and calls `log.warn()`
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts lines 47-52: setSessionStorage catches errors and calls `log.warn()`
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts lines 59-65: removeLocalStorage catches errors and calls `log.warn()`
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts lines 71-77: removeSessionStorage catches errors and calls `log.warn()`
- Note: getLocalStorage and getSessionStorage truly return null silently (they don't log), so only the set/remove functions need updating.

Current docs state for storage (around lines 489-525):
- setLocalStorage: 'Silently fails if storage access throws (SecurityError or QuotaExceededError)'
- setSessionStorage: 'Silently fails if storage access throws (SecurityError or QuotaExceededError)'
- removeLocalStorage: 'Silently fails if storage access throws'
- removeSessionStorage: 'Silently fails if storage access throws'

Source of truth for httpStatusToToolError:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts lines 15-40: Full status code switch. Currently documented table (around line 437) is missing:
  - 400, 422 → validation category, retryable false
  - 408 → timeout category, retryable true
- Insert new rows in the Table component between existing rows. The existing table uses a RetroUI Table component with <Table.Row>/<Table.Cell> elements.

userStories.title: Add port field to configuration reference
userStories.description: As a user, I want the configuration reference to document the `port` field in config.json so that I know I can persist a custom port in my config file.
acceptanceCriteria: The config fields table in configuration.mdx includes a row for `port` with type `number`, default `9515` (or 'omitted — uses default 9515'), and a description explaining it sets the MCP server port
The description notes that when set to the default value (9515), the field is removed from config.json (matching the implementation behavior)
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in configuration.mdx is set to today's date (2026-02-28)
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx

Source of truth:
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts lines 370-389: handleSetPort function. When newPort === DEFAULT_PORT, it does `delete config.port`; otherwise sets `config.port = newPort`. This means port is only present in config.json when it differs from the default.
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 59: `const port = typeof config.port === 'number' ? config.port : DEFAULT_PORT;`
- platform/shared/src/constants.ts: DEFAULT_PORT = 9515

The config fields table starts around line 17 of configuration.mdx and uses a RetroUI Table component. Add a new row for `port` following the same pattern as existing rows (`localPlugins`, `tools`, `browserToolPolicy`, `permissions`, `skipConfirmation`).

Note: the lastUpdated frontmatter is currently 2026-02-28 already, but verify and update if needed.

userStories.title: Document displayName and version properties in plugin development guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the getting-started guide, I want the class properties section to describe displayName and version so that I understand these are required fields.
acceptanceCriteria: The class properties description list in plugin-development.mdx includes `displayName` with a description explaining it is the human-readable name shown in the side panel and health endpoint
The class properties description list includes `version` with a description explaining it is the plugin version string (e.g., semver)
Both are described alongside the existing documented properties (`name`, `urlPatterns`, `tools`, `isReady()`)
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in plugin-development.mdx is set to today's date (2026-02-28)
userStories.notes:
File to edit: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx

Source of truth:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 222: `abstract readonly displayName: string;` with JSDoc: 'Human-readable display name shown in the side panel and health endpoint'
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 194: `abstract readonly version: string;` — required abstract property
- Also see docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx lines 18-37 for the full class signature (this is the reference page that already documents these correctly as abstract properties)

Current state (plugin-development.mdx around lines 87-92):
The text describes `name`, `urlPatterns`, `tools`, and `isReady()` but omits `displayName`, `version`, and `description`. The example code (lines 71-84) shows all properties including displayName and version, but the explanatory bullet points below don't mention them.

Add bullet points for `displayName`, `version`, and `description` in the same style as the existing bullets. Also add `description` since it's an abstract required property that's also missing from the text.
